title: He’s running his 11th Boston Marathon for Boston Children’s Hospital
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Steven Iannacone is running his 11th Boston Marathon in 2026 for Boston Children's Hospital's Miles for Miracles program.
Iannacone runs in honor of Jace Perkins, who was born prematurely and treated at Boston Children's Hospital.
Jace Perkins, now 12, received critical care at the hospital, thriving with the help of NICU and neurology teams.

title: Did you follow the local news this week? Take our Greater Boston news quiz.
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: Boston.com offers a quiz to test knowledge of recent Greater Boston news.
Peter Chianca is the general assignment editor at Boston.com since 2019.
The quiz aims to keep readers informed about trending stories in the area.

title: Red Sox took advantage of MLB’s new automated strike challenge rules on Opening Day
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Red Sox used MLB's new Automated Ball-Strike Challenge System in their 3-0 win over the Reds.
Catcher Carlos Narvaez successfully challenged a crucial fourth-inning call, helping maintain Garrett Crochet's shutout.
Roman Anthony's ninth-inning challenge led to a walk, enabling Boston to extend their lead with subsequent RBIs.

title: In first Opening Day with Red Sox, Marcelo Mayer made quite the impression off the bench
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Marcelo Mayer, 23, made his impactful Opening Day debut for the Red Sox, entering as a pinch-hitter and going 2-for-2.
Red Sox manager Alex Cora emphasized competition, keeping Mayer on the roster despite previous offensive struggles and injury woes.
Mayer's contributions helped secure a 3-0 win over the Reds, breaking a scoreless tie in the seventh inning with a leadoff double.

title: Lucky pup thanks NH firefighters for quick thinking that saved his life
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Ziggy the dog was saved by Campton-Thornton firefighters after cutting his leg on a solar light.
Firefighters applied pressure and used a tourniquet to stop Ziggy's bleeding before sending him to an animal hospital.
Veterinarian confirmed that firefighter intervention was crucial in preventing Ziggy from dying due to excessive blood loss.

title: What’s causing my car to burn so much oil?
author: John Paul
bullets: A 2019 Subaru Legacy with 89,000 miles is experiencing excessive oil consumption and Subaru recommended replacing the short block.
John Paul suggests checking the PCV system and using engine flushes as alternatives to replacing the short block.
A new short block replacement could be a worthwhile investment given high vehicle prices and the car's otherwise good condition.

title: This Nantucket diner just sold its fifth million dollar prize in the last 2 years
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: The Old South Diner in Nantucket sold its fifth lottery ticket worth over $1 million in two years.
This month, the diner sold two $2 million prize tickets, both announced by state lottery officials.
The diner receives a $20,000 bonus from the Massachusetts State Lottery Commission for selling each winning ticket.

title: School bus driver in crash that killed child was allegedly involved in hit-and-run months later
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Jean Charles, 39, was arraigned on involuntary manslaughter and vehicle homicide charges in the death of a 5-year-old boy.
Charles allegedly failed a safety inspection and had a broken crossing bar and flat tire on the bus during the crash.
Months after the fatal accident, Charles was allegedly involved in a hit-and-run and charged with leaving the scene.

title: 5 takeaways as Garrett Crochet, Marcelo Mayer steer Red Sox to 3-0 Opening Day win over Reds
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: Garrett Crochet pitched six scoreless innings, allowing three hits and striking out eight, helping the Red Sox to a 3-0 victory.
Marcelo Mayer's pivotal double in the seventh inning set the tone for the Red Sox's scoring breakthrough.
Justin Slaten, Garrett Whitlock, and Aroldis Chapman each pitched a scoreless inning to secure the Red Sox's season-opening win.

title: Body of Mass. man missing since escaping from a hospital in 2025 found in lake
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Leonard Mercury, 57, missing since October 2025, was found in Crystal Lake, Gardner, five months after his escape from Heywood Hospital.
Mercury, from Acton, broke a hospital window and escaped without shoes or adequate clothing, according to his wife, Nicole La Guerre.
Gardner police used drones to locate Mercury's body, and the State Police Dive Team confirmed the identity; investigation is ongoing.
